---
title: "HONR 46400: Evidence-Driven Research"
subtitle: "Honors College, Purdue University (Fall 2026 — Mon/Wed/Fri)"
---

<br>

<center>

*IMPORTANT*

*This document does not replace the official material in the course Brightspace page*

</center>

<br>

## Course Description and Objectives

This Honors seminar teaches you to turn curiosity into credible, evidence-based insight — **no strong background in quantitative methods or computing required**. You will learn to identify meaningful gaps in a field you care about, translate a gap into a well-scoped research problem, and formulate answerable research questions. You will then learn — at a **conceptual** level — how to choose and justify an appropriate method family (description, statistical inference, predictive modeling, and/or causal reasoning), and how to use everyday computational tools and AI to locate sources, operationalize concepts, analyze evidence, verify results, and document your decisions responsibly. By the end you will design and defend a rigorous research approach, interpret findings with appropriate uncertainty and limitations, and communicate your results effectively in both **written and oral** formats.

**Course Website**: [https://davi-moreira.github.io/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/](https://davi-moreira.github.io/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/){target="_blank"}

### Instructor: [Professor Davi Moreira](https://davi-moreira.github.io)

-   Email: [dcordeir\@purdue.edu](mailto:dcordeir@purdue.edu)
-   Class meetings: **Monday / Wednesday / Friday** — in person (time and room on Brightspace)
-   Office Hours: *See the syllabus on your Course Brightspace Page, or by appointment.*

## Learning Outcomes

By the conclusion of this course, you will be able to:

1.  **Identify a gap and scope a problem:** Locate a meaningful gap in a chosen field and translate it into a well-scoped, researchable problem.

2.  **Formulate answerable research questions:** Turn a problem into questions that evidence can actually address, at an appropriate scope.

3.  **Select and justify a method family conceptually:** Choose among description, statistical inference, predictive modeling, and causal reasoning, and justify the choice and its limitations without heavy mathematics.

4.  **Locate, appraise, and analyze evidence responsibly with AI:** Use computational tools and AI to find sources, operationalize concepts, analyze evidence, and **verify** results — documenting every decision.

5.  **Interpret and communicate with uncertainty:** Report findings with appropriate uncertainty and limitations, effectively, in **written and oral** formats.

## Course Materials

-   **Readings:** The course's primary text is **Blair, Coppock & Humphreys (2023), *Research Design in the Social Sciences: Declaration, Diagnosis, and Redesign*** (Princeton University Press) — read **free online** at [book.declaredesign.org](https://book.declaredesign.org/){target="_blank"}; no purchase required. Chapters are read conceptually (no quantitative prerequisite), on the light cadence in the course schedule. Optional companion: Bergstrom & West, *Calling Bullshit* (public case studies at [callingbullshit.org](https://callingbullshit.org/){target="_blank"}). Any additional readings are posted on Brightspace.

-   **Computing (Required):** A laptop or desktop with internet access and a modern web browser. Course activities run in the browser through [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/){target="_blank"} — no local installation required.

-   **AI + Tools:**

    -   **A general-purpose AI assistant** (e.g., [Google Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/){target="_blank"} / Microsoft Copilot) used under the course's **Ask → Verify → Document** policy: draft with AI assistance, verify every claim and source against real, retrievable evidence, and document your decisions. You own every claim you submit.
    -   [**Microsoft Copilot**](https://it.purdue.edu/newsroom/2024/240313-copilot-with-data-protection.php){target="_blank"} is available to Purdue students with data protection, for drafting, summarizing, and analyzing within a privacy-conscious framework.

## Course Infra-structure

**Brightspace:** The Course Brightspace Page <https://purdue.brightspace.com/> should be checked on a regular basis for announcements and course material.
